title: Obamacare rolls shrank dramatically in many states over the past year, new federal data shows
summary: Federal data reveals approximately 2.6 million fewer Americans had ACA coverage in February 2026 compared to the prior year, following the January expiration of enhanced subsidies. Ohio and Oklahoma each lost nearly one-third of their enrollees, marking sharp nationwide declines in Affordable Care Act enrollment.
analysis_notes: Full article body was behind a paywall; summary derived from headline, two lead paragraphs, and contextual links. Key missing details include full state-by-state data and specific subsidy amounts discussed in the paid content.
